content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

The PID liveness implementation is correct and well-designed. The three-state PidRecordLiveState enum (Live/Stale/RunningUnverified) properly addresses all acceptance criteria: (1) macOS/BSD correctly uses proc_start_marker via identity_is_authoritative and process_identity_is_alive on non-Linux unix; (2) legacy pid files without start-time fields return RunningUnverified instead of assuming alive or dead; (3) PID reuse is detected by comparing live process start time against recorded started_at via compare_legacy_pid_record; (4) the WholeSeconds precision correctly handles the 1-second ambiguity window on macOS/BSD. All four call sites (classify_running_snapshot_liveness, orchestrator_process_is_alive, interrupted_handoff_cleanup_candidate, run_pid_record_is_reclaimable) correctly treat RunningUnverified conservatively — blocking reclaim and treating the process as running. Tests cover legacy records without start time, same-second ambiguity, unambiguously later recovery, and integration with run.rs and cli_writer_lease.rs. All tests pass, clippy is clean.

## Amendments

None.
